meta.name: PHP Attacks
meta.description: PHP-specific attack payloads including RCE via dangerous functions, type juggling exploits, LFI/RFI, object injection via unserialize, and stream wrapper abuse.

payloads.description: Classic PHP webshell — executes OS command from ?cmd= GET parameter
shell_exec webshell — returns full command output as string
Passthru webshell — raw command output, useful for binary data
Eval-based backdoor — executes base64-encoded PHP code from POST parameter
PHP file read — reads /etc/passwd without exec functions
PHP loose comparison quirk — '0'=='a' evaluates TRUE (PHP 5/7); use === to fix
Magic hash collision — both hash to 0e...; PHP compares as 0==0 (scientific notation)
Numeric string comparison — 0=='foobar' is TRUE in PHP 5-7 (string cast to 0)
Type confusion via JSON decode — input typed as string may bypass strict type checks
strcmp() bypass — pass an array: strcmp(Array, string) returns NULL, NULL==0 is TRUE
PHP filter LFI — base64-encodes target file to avoid null bytes or special chars
PHP filter with ROT13 transform — alternative encoding for LFI bypass
PHP data:// stream wrapper — base64 decodes to <?php system($_GET['cmd']); ?>
PHP expect:// wrapper — executes command directly (requires expect extension)
PHP zip:// wrapper — reads shell.php from inside shell.jpg archive
PHP phar:// wrapper — executes PHP inside a PHAR-disguised file
Remote file inclusion — requires allow_url_include=On (PHP ≤5.6 default)
RFI via UNC path — includes PHP from Windows share if allow_url_include is on
PHP object injection — serialized User object with role=admin; fed to unserialize()
PHP serialized array injection — admin username with empty password
Gadget chain seed — object with file property pointing to sensitive file
PHP ArrayObject deserialization gadget — used in some framework chains
preg_replace /e modifier RCE — deprecated PHP 5.5, removed PHP 7; eval's replacement
create_function() RCE — effectively eval(); removed in PHP 8.0
call_user_func injection — if func and arg are user-controlled, call any PHP function
assert() RCE in PHP 5 — treated as eval() when passed a string
